old_claim.note: Tribalism provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: Social differentiation or stratification is identified as an essential condition for slavery to flourish.

source_shape.source_locator: Britannica says slavery was rare among hunter-gatherer societies because social differentiation or stratification was essential for slavery to flourish.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source identifies social stratification as an essential condition and explicitly contrasts it with hunter-gatherer societies.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The replacement points the graph at the source-named social condition for slavery, while the old kin-based band edge pointed at the type of society the source says was usually unfavorable to slavery.

ontology_before: Chattel Slavery depended on Kin-Based Band Organization, implying small gatherer-hunter kin organization enabled legal chattel slavery.
ontology_after: Chattel Slavery depends on Social Stratification as the source-backed social hierarchy condition for slavery to flourish.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: slavery_chattel now depends on social_stratification and no longer directly depends on tribalism.
would_reject_if: The node were later rescoped to small-scale captive adoption inside kin-band societies rather than legal chattel slavery.
A stronger source showed kin-based band organization directly enabled the Old Babylonian legal-property slavery scope.
The social_stratification node were rescoped away from hierarchy, wealth, power, and labor-status differentiation.
short_reason: The source supports stratification, not kin-band organization, as the relevant social condition.
